title: iberis intermedia var. maialis
intro: Iberis intermedia var. maialis, also known as Candytuft or Rock Candy, is a flowering plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white, pink, or purple flowers and its ability to thrive in well-drained soils and full sun. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Iberis intermedia var. maialis has been linked to several health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and diuretic. Iberis intermedia var. maialis can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
